_note: GENUINE news-derived seeds (real URLs, real ledes verbatim from the source articles) for the GDX test. KEY seed = tradingnews (2026-01-03), the first genuine article that NAMES GDX by ticker with a forward 'room to run' thesis ($85.73 -> $100+ target), planted as the trigger per the halfway-naming logic: the press named GDX at ~halfway with ~+35% still to run to the Feb-27-2026 ~$116 peak. Saxo + Nation Thailand add the dollar-risk context + the Greenland de-escalation exit signal (both name gold, not GDX). This tests whether the scout will name GDX once the press hands it the ticker (vs the earlier gold-only seeds it ignored). Look-ahead caveat: curator training likely postdates these dates (upper bound, non-negotiable #4). Non-blank url = news-derived (vs synthetic seeds elsewhere).

articles.title: GDX ETF At $85 After 155% 2025 Rally - Can Gold Miners Smash The $100 Level Next?
articles.snippet: With gold up 65% in 2025, GDX riding record AISC spreads and majors like NEM, AEM and Barrick minting cash, investors are betting the gold-miner trade isn't finished until VanEck's GDX ETF clears $100. NYSEARCA:GDX trades around $85.73, sitting close to the upper end of a brutal 12-month range between $34.53 and $91.66. Using the depth of the October-November 2025 selloff as the base of the move, the measured-move target sits around $102, putting a logical upside objective just above the prior $91.66 high and into triple-digit territory.

articles.title: Trump's tariff threats over Greenland push hard assets back to centre stage
articles.snippet: Gold and silver, which both faced some profit-taking pressure late last week, jumped to fresh record highs as renewed geopolitical tensions resurfaced. Trump's push to take control of Greenland has widened the geopolitical fault line between the United States and Europe and introduced the risk of tariffs and retaliation. Gold is the first and most obvious beneficiary of this mindset - it has no credit risk, no issuer, and a long history as a store of value during periods of political and monetary uncertainty.

articles.title: Gold cools after Trump drops Europe tariff threat on Greenland
articles.snippet: Global gold prices cooled on Wednesday (Jan 21, 2026), easing from a fresh all-time high after US President Donald Trump backed away from some of his tougher threats linked to the dispute over Greenland, prompting some selling in gold. The metal had reached $4,887.82/oz earlier in the session but settled lower at $4,778.51/oz as Trump announced a Greenland framework agreement with NATO.
